nodes.label: ∅ | lie | cabin
nodes.sentence: In a distant corner of the dense Germanic woods lay a lonely forester’s cabin, home to a renowned hunter named Dietrich, his fair wife Elsa, and their two sons, Wilhelm and Otto.

nodes.label: elsa | remain | seclusion
nodes.sentence: Each day, as Dietrich ventured into the vast Schwarzwald to track deer and boar—their chief sustenance—Elsa and her sons remained in the seclusion of their timbered home.

nodes.label: game | be | day
nodes.sentence: In those days, game was plentiful, and Dietrich’s skilled pursuit brought them well-fed and secure.

nodes.label: homestead | remove | village
nodes.sentence: Their homestead was far removed from other villages, and rarely did any stranger’s face breach the wild domain beyond their clearing.

nodes.label: they | roam | ∅
nodes.sentence: Wilhelm and Otto were yet too young to share their father’s hunts; instead, they roamed nearby, chasing woodland creatures and watching birds flit through the ancient oaks and pines.

nodes.label: it | be | wandering
nodes.sentence: It was during these innocent wanderings that they noticed a tall stranger, a somber young man, visiting the cabin repeatedly whenever their father was away.

nodes.label: visit | be | whisper
nodes.sentence: The visits were quiet and frequent, like whispers between shadows.

nodes.label: wilhelm | turn | mother
nodes.sentence: One evening, Wilhelm turned to their mother, curiosity shining in his eyes.

nodes.label: he | seek | father
nodes.sentence: Does he seek my father?

nodes.label: i | tell | father
nodes.sentence:
Shall I tell Father when he returns?”

Elsa’s face tightened like the bark of an old tree.

nodes.label: she | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Foolish child,” she said sharply.

nodes.label: ∅ | attend | bow
nodes.sentence: “Attend to your bows and arrows.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Be brave and venture into the woods with your brother.

nodes.label: son | linger | idle
nodes.sentence: A hunter’s sons do not linger at the hearth idle, nor do they speak of every shadow they glimpse.

nodes.label: ∅ | say | nothing
nodes.sentence: Your father has enough to bear; say nothing.”

nodes.label: boy | obey | ∅
nodes.sentence: The boys obeyed, yet their suspicions grew as the stranger’s visits endured.

nodes.label: they | press | ∅
nodes.sentence: Now older, they pressed again, declaring their intent to tell their father, for the man never followed paths nor carried food, and if he bore news, why did he never speak to their father directly?

nodes.label: they | mention | stranger
nodes.sentence: Their mother’s countenance darkened to storm clouds; she threatened them with terrible consequences should they mention the stranger.

nodes.label: they | keep | silent
nodes.sentence: Fearing her wrath, they kept silent for a time.

nodes.label: visitor | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: Yet, the shadowy visitor came still, weaving curiosity into dread.

nodes.label: they | confide | father
nodes.sentence: At last, they confided all to their father during a hunting lesson beneath towering firs.

nodes.label: eye | blaze | fury
nodes.sentence: Dietrich’s eyes blazed with silent fury before he steadied himself.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “It is decided,” he said grimly.

nodes.label: you | remain | ∅
nodes.sentence: “You two remain here until sundown and then return to the cabin.

nodes.label: you | find | i
nodes.sentence: You shall find me there.”

nodes.label: he | walk | ∅
nodes.sentence: He walked away slowly, leaving the boys to their restless play until twilight summoned them home.

nodes.label: they | find | arrival
nodes.sentence: Upon arrival, they found the cabin empty.

nodes.label: mother | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Their mother was gone—and never again was her name spoken in the hearthlit walls.

nodes.label: elsa | linger | curse
nodes.sentence: Though unseen, Elsa lingered like a curse, a withered specter with hollow eyes and gnarled limbs, appearing at dusk to haunt her sons as they returned from the hunt.

nodes.label: she | unbolt | door
nodes.sentence: By night, she would silently unbolt the door and drift through their slumbering forms, mocking them with her pale, bony visage suspended in the darkness.

nodes.label: presence | turn | home
nodes.sentence: Her dreadful presence turned their once-familiar home into a place of terror.

nodes.label: ∅ | torment | endurance
nodes.sentence: Tormented beyond endurance, the family—father and now grown sons—resolved to flee the cursed forest.

nodes.label: path | lead | shore
nodes.sentence: Their path led southward, following the winding shore of a vast, mist-shrouded lake.

nodes.label: they | come | cliff
nodes.sentence: Around a jagged cliff, they came upon a raging waterfall where the lake gave birth to a mighty river, thundering into the valley below.

nodes.label: skull | roll | shore
nodes.sentence: As they gazed upon the churning cascade, a strange sight startled them—a woman’s skull rolling swiftly along the rocky shore, as if in pursuit.

nodes.label: ∅ | emerge | figure
nodes.sentence: From the nearby forest emerged a headless figure, moving with grim purpose toward the skull.

nodes.label: part | merge | speed
nodes.sentence: The two parts merged with unnatural speed, forming a dreadful phantom racing after the hunter and his sons.

nodes.label: fear | seize | they
nodes.sentence: Fear seized them, for the haunt was relentless, and escape seemed impossible.

nodes.label: feather | dull | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then Wilhelm spotted a great crane perched solemnly upon a rock amid the foaming rapids—a venerable bird, feathers dulled by countless winters, eyes deep with ancient wisdom.

nodes.label: crane | help | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Grandfather crane,” they called, “help us cross the falls and flee this evil!”

nodes.label: crane | stretch | neck
nodes.sentence: The crane, immense and solemn, stretched forth its long neck, rose on mighty wings, and flew across the turbulent water to their aid.

nodes.label: ∅ | beware | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Beware,” the old bird warned, “touch not the crown of my head.

nodes.label: age | leave | it
nodes.sentence: Age has left it bare and tender.

nodes.label: i | save | you
nodes.sentence: Should you lay hands upon it, I cannot save you from the raging currents below.”

nodes.label: they | heed | word
nodes.sentence: They heeded his words with reverence, and soon found themselves safe upon the far bank.

nodes.label: crane | return | ∅
nodes.sentence: The crane returned and carried Dietrich in like fashion before settling once more among the restless rapids.

nodes.label: woman | reach | shore
nodes.sentence: But the woman—now whole and dreadful—reached the shore and cried, “Grandfather, carry me across, that I may be with my children.

nodes.label: i | lose | ∅
nodes.sentence: I am lost and sorrowful.”

nodes.label: crane | caution | she
nodes.sentence: The aged crane cautioned her again, repeating his warning with great solemnity.

nodes.label: she | vow | ∅
nodes.sentence: She vowed to obey, but scarcely had they begun the crossing than she slyly sought to touch the fragile crown.

nodes.label: crane | cast | she
nodes.sentence: Swift as the storm wind, the crane cast her into the frothing torrent.

nodes.label: you | do | forbidden
nodes.sentence: “There,” he spoke sternly as she sank beneath the whirlpool, “you have done the forbidden once more.

nodes.label: you | pursue | those
nodes.sentence: As in life you pursued those to be shunned, so shall you henceforth be shunned by those who seek you.

nodes.label: woman | lose | time
nodes.sentence: Consumed by the river’s depths, the ghostly woman was lost to all time and memory.

nodes.label: family | embrace | crane
nodes.sentence: Grateful and wise, the hunter’s family embraced the crane as their talisman, a symbol of protection and endurance.

nodes.label: they | prosper | wing
nodes.sentence: Under the watchful wings of the Crane that Crossed the River, they prospered still—surrounded by forest peace, abundant game, and the blessings of the ancient wild.
